body:
## Summary

This is about wether we should allow developers to install components individually on npm or not:

- `@base-ui-components/react-slider`
- `@base-ui-components/react-dialog`
- etc.

## Motivation

I'm not suggesting that we should do it, I'm only trying to create a place where we can discuss this with the community, and give them our stance on the problem. For example: https://youtu.be/lIUp8bdKiq4?si=SY5s4xthHufJsrHJ&t=1490 from @t3dotgg.

To mind that Shadcn UI is moving in the opposite direction: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/issues/6428


## Context

A bit related to #601.

comments.body:
We discussed this in detail before deciding on the monopackage, and I don't think the community will be asking for this

1. It's largely annoying to have to install multiple packages when you realize you need another component
2. Treeshaking and sub-path imports eliminate issues associated with monopackages
3. There are many issues that arise when using separate packages: https://bsky.app/profile/haz.dev/post/3lckcvhdn2s2a

As a consumer of Base UI, please don't do this.

I used Angular Material for over 4 years without an issue. I started using Radix 4 months ago and have already been hit with bugs due to mismatched packages.

Currently using AntD. Considering using base-ui, when it comes out of beta, currently targeting radix-ui. 

Would really like to see separate packages, currently one of the biggest pain points for our team is inability to update just one component to receive some bug fixes. Currently we have to update the whole AntD package to, for example, fix a popover behavior, but the update breaks the date picker instead.

This means we have to thoroughly test every component in our quite large application to see if updating the caused anything else to break / not work as we expected prior to the update.

Separate packages is one of the main selling points of radix-ui for us

labels.name: waiting for 👍
labels.description: Waiting for upvotes. Open for community feedback and needs more interest to be worked on.

labels.name: scope: all components
labels.description: Widespread work has an impact on almost all components.

title: [discussion] Support individual npm package install?
